topic: Hubble Law aetheric — c5 verification of ISS-1994 geometry fix (30,000 km → 40,030 km disc diameter) across WIN-047/052/054 sibling patches
change_summary: Verification pass on decider self-apply (closed-issues ISS-1994, fixed_at 2026-05-13T09:21:39Z). WIN-047 tldr_verdict_length 388→415 (+7%); WIN-052 tldr_verdict and WIN-054 detail_verdict_text also rewritten per the c4 moderate finding. detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text on WIN-047 unchanged.
our_argument_summary: Post-ISS-1994 self-apply, WIN-047 tldr_verdict reads "bounded firmament (disc diameter 40,030 km, firmament height 9,086 km)" and "The dome cannot publish a disc diameter of 40,030 km while citing measurements at galactic distances as confirmation." The erroneous r+h=29,101 km framing is gone from all three sibling WINs. The Self-Contradicted verdict remains correct and the SC structural argument is unchanged in substance — only the geometric anchor numbers were swapped from the wrong figure to the dome+'+s published headline disc diameter.

relevant_files: raw-text/05-model.txt (disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km — dome+'+s published parameters)
dome_code_analysis.note: Verification-class review. Code-tag analysis carried from c2/c4 unchanged — the patch only touched prose, not code behavior or tags.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new. The dome+'+s 40,030 km disc diameter (= 2 × 20,015 km disc_radius) is the dome+'+s own published figure — patching to that value removes the unforced rhetorical opening that r+h=29,101 km had created.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Carried from c2/c4 — monitor.py contains no domain that tracks H_0 itself; the live-power dashboard’s Aetheric Redshift Scale domain compares an aetheric-scale parameter to a Virgo measurement, not H_0 to a derivation. Verification pass did not re-inspect monitor.py; no code changes were part of this patch.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Carried from c2/c4 — H_0 = 67-74 km/s/Mpc is textbook standard cosmology; the dome adopts the range without producing a different numerical prediction.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Carried from c2/c4 — Hubble’s Law dates to 1929; modern H_0 measurements since SH0ES/Planck are decades-old. Dome’s prediction is the published modern value restated.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Carried from c2/c4 — no derivation chain from disc_radius/firmament_height to H_0. The aetheric wavelength lambda_A = 4283 Mpc that appears in monitor.py is c/H_0 = 4282.7 Mpc — adopted, not derived (cross-ref WIN-054).

stronger_arguments.description: Now that the dome+'+s correct headline disc diameter (40,030 km) anchors the SC argument, the curmudgeon c4 suggestion to make the dimensional ratio explicit becomes more powerful: at 40,030 km horizontal extent the dome is still 18-19 orders of magnitude short of the cosmological distances WIN-047 cites (Pantheon+ z=2.26 ~1.73×10^26 m → 18.6 orders of magnitude). WIN-054 already does this for El Gordo (eighteen orders of magnitude); WIN-047 could borrow the same framing in its detail_verdict_text. Not blocking; deferred from c4.

The c4 stronger_arguments suggestion to cite the dome’s own live-power Aetheric Redshift Scale domain (raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 236-238 — Virgo cluster NED measurement at ~16 Mpc against an aetheric-scale parameter) inline in WIN-047 detail_evidence is still not implemented. The current detail_evidence_length is 1776 (unchanged from c4). This would be a 1-2 sentence addition that hardens the SC argument with a verbatim dome-source quote. Not blocking; deferred to a future BAU patch round.
sources: raw-text/08-live-power.txt:236-238 (Aetheric Redshift Scale domain — Predicted 4283 Mpc, Observed 4319 Mpc, Source: Virgo cluster NED)

kernel_of_truth.description: Verification-class review — no new kernel insight beyond what c4 documented. The dome author’s instinct to highlight the Hubble tension as an opening for alternative models remains a defensible move in principle (data/wins.json:WIN-047:detail_evidence). The ISS-1994 self-apply has not weakened this kernel — it has only corrected the geometric anchor on our own side of the argument.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Carried from c4: the dome offers no derivation pathway from its own parameters to either competing Hubble value, and its bounded-firmament parameter forecloses the spatial framework in which any such derivation could be tested. The post-patch text now states this correctly against the dome’s 40,030 km disc diameter (data/wins.json:WIN-047:tldr_verdict) rather than the previously-wrong r+h=29,101 km figure — the argument is materially unchanged but the geometric anchor is now the dome’s own published headline number, which closes the rhetorical opening the c4 advocate-mode defense had identified.

advocate_mode.best_defense: The strongest remaining dome-defender rebuttal at c5: "You corrected the 30,000 km error to 40,030 km — fine. But the cross-reference to a non-existent Part 6.13 still sits in WIN-047.sub_rationale (ISS-2002 / ISS-1922 unresolved). If you cannot keep your own internal references coherent across a verdict-reclassification patch, why should a reader trust your structural-coherence critique of our model?" This is not a strong defense — sub_rationale is currently audit-trail-only and not rendered to readers — but it is the residual attack surface after ISS-1994 closes. ISS-1922 routing to analyst is the right channel; no curmudgeon action needed in this verification pass.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: None for this verification pass. The ISS-1994 fix lands cleanly; the residual sub_rationale Part 6.13 dangling reference is already tracked at ISS-1922 (assigned-analyst) and ISS-2002 as a c4 carry-over. The c4 finding text awkwardness (semicolon-chained finding) is tracked at ISS-2003 (assigned-analyst). No new patches needed at c5.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: Post-ISS-1994 self-apply, WIN-047, WIN-052 (tldr_verdict), and WIN-054 (detail_verdict_text) all now use the correct 40,030 km disc diameter (and/or 20,015 km radius + 9,086 km firmament height) as the geometric anchor for the bounded-firmament argument. The propagated patch error from EXP-295 sibling integration is fully corrected across all three WINs. No new cross-WIN inconsistencies introduced. (Sanity check: grep -n "30,000 km\|29,101" data/wins.json data/sections.json filtered to bounded/max/spatial-extent contexts returns zero hits.)

claims_checked: Dome disc diameter 40,030 km (= 2 × 20,015 km disc_radius)
Corner-to-corner diagonal ~41,048 km (not used in patched text)
WIN-054 “eighteen orders of magnitude” — recomputed against disc diameter
Pantheon+ z=2.26 comoving ~1.73×10^26 m vs 4.003×10^7 m disc diameter → 18.6 orders of magnitude
quantitative_verification.computations: r=20,015 km, h=9,086 km. 2r=40,030 km (disc diameter — current patched anchor). r+h=29,101 km (the “30,000” figure the c4 review flagged — now removed from all three WINs). sqrt((2r)^2+h^2)=41,048 km (corner diagonal — not used). El Gordo z=0.87 ~4.6×10^25 m: log10(4.6e25 / 4.003e7) = 18.06 orders of magnitude against disc diameter (rounds to eighteen — preserves WIN-054 prose). log10(4.6e25 / 9.086e6) = 18.70 against firmament height (also rounds to eighteen — WIN-054’s “maximum extent” phrasing is consistent either way). Pantheon+ z=2.26 ~1.73×10^26 m: log10(1.73e26 / 4.003e7) = 18.6 orders of magnitude. WIN-052 RAR at 1-100 kpc (3.086e19 to 3.086e21 m): log10 vs disc diameter = 11.9 to 13.9 orders of magnitude — current WIN-052 tldr_verdict does not quote an explicit order-of-magnitude figure, so no recompute needed.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: None. The geometric anchors in the patched prose match the dome’s own published parameter table. Order-of-magnitude claims preserve their rounded values when re-benchmarked against the new disc-diameter anchor.

summary_for_decider: VERIFICATION PASS — queue_id 344. ISS-1994 self-apply (geometry fix 30,000 km → 40,030 km disc diameter) landed cleanly across WIN-047 tldr_verdict (both occurrences), WIN-052 tldr_verdict, and WIN-054 detail_verdict_text. text_fingerprint matches the expected post-patch state (only WIN-047 tldr_verdict_length changed, 388→415, consistent with the geometry anchor substitution). Verdict Self-Contradicted holds unchanged. Order-of-magnitude claims in WIN-054 preserve their rounded values when re-benchmarked. No new holes found; no new ISSs needed. recommended_action=no_change. Pre-existing minor open issues from c4 (ISS-2002 sub_rationale dangling, ISS-2003 finding awkward opening) remain assigned-analyst and are not in scope for this verification — leave them in their current routing. queue_id 344 can be popped with no further work.
deeper_analysis: This is a clean verification-class outcome. The c4 review identified a propagated patch error (EXP-295 sibling patches used r+h=29,101 km as a geometric anchor across WIN-047/052/054, which was a category error — adding a radius to a height is not a spatial extent). The decider self-applied a single-issue fix (ISS-1994) on 2026-05-13T09:21:39Z replacing the wrong anchor with the dome’s own published disc diameter (40,030 km = 2 × disc_radius 20,015 km). c5 verification confirms: (a) all three WINs now use the correct anchor; (b) no residual “30,000 km” or “29,101 km” references remain in bounded/max/spatial-extent contexts; (c) the SC argument is materially preserved — actually rhetorically stronger, since 40,030 km is the dome’s own headline number and the 18-19 orders of magnitude gap to cosmological distances is unchanged; (d) WIN-054’s “eighteen orders of magnitude” prose still rounds correctly when re-benchmarked against disc diameter (18.06) rather than firmament height (18.70); (e) the c4 advocate-mode defense (“you misrepresent our geometry”) is fully neutralized. The cross-WIN consistency mechanism — which is what caught the propagated error in the first place — also catches that the fix propagated correctly across all three siblings. This is the intended use of verification-class queue items: a focused 1-hole confirmation that the self-apply did what the curmudgeon flagged, without re-litigating the underlying verdict or re-running deep adversarial argumentation. Single-item, no batching applicable (queue had only this one item). Two carry-over minors (ISS-2002, ISS-2003) remain assigned-analyst and are correctly outside the verification scope.
